page: Microsoft Azure E8ads_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EADSv5 family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The instance features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled, providing 307 GB of local disk capacity, and the instance is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 25%. Multi-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU scores, and Geekbench multi-core metrics fall within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests reveal a distinct tradeoff: while small-block cached read operations achieve an average speed of 515.05 GB/sec, large-block uncached read and write operations are weak, scoring in the bottom 25% at 38.15 GB/sec and 22.67 GB/sec respectively.

This server is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and caching layers, as demonstrated by its average performance of 8.2 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and stable database operations. It offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy applications due to its high resource density of 8 GB of RAM per vCPU and dedicated CPU allocation, though its performance on larger computational tasks like 7B LLM prompt processing is limited.
description: Microsoft Azure E8ads_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, utilizing an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture with Microsoft Hyper-V, it features 4 physical cores, 32 MB of L3 cache, and 307 GB of local storage. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU capabilities, though multi-core execution and uncached memory bandwidth fall into average and weak categories respectively. The instance delivers average throughput for web serving, Redis operations, and software compilation. Given its high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU and local storage capacity, this server is designed for memory-intensive workloads, relational databases, and caching systems that require dedicated compute resources without the need for hardware acceleration.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8ads_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and 307 GB of local storage for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8ads_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 8 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 307 GB local storage, suited for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, high memory density, and local storage for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz
Configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory
Includes 307 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage
Delivers strong single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
Exhibits lower bandwidth for large-block uncached memory operations
Suited for memory-intensive databases, caching, and web serving
